title: Electric generator for electric vehicle
abstract: A vehicle includes a first prime mover and a first prime mover driven transmission. A vehicle drive system for the vehicle includes an electric energy source, an electric motor in direct or indirect mechanical communication with the first prime mover, and a high power generator coupled to the electric motor. The electric motor is configured to generate energy for storage in the energy source via rotation using power from the first prime mover and drives the generator using energy from the electric energy source.
claims:
Claims 1. A vehicle drive system for a vehicle comprising a first prime mover and a first prime mover driven transmission, the vehicle drive system comprising: an electric energy source; an electric motor in direct or indirect mechanical communication with the first prime mover; and a high power generator coupled to the electric motor, wherein the electric motor is configured to generate energy for storage in the energy source via rotation using power from the first prime mover and drives the generator using energy from the electric energy source. 2. The vehicle drive system of claim 1, wherein a control system is configured to cause the electric motor to rotate at a selected speed for driving the generator. 3. The vehicle drive system of claim 1, wherein the electric motor in direct or indirect mechanical communication with the first prime mover is mounted to a PTO. 4. The vehicle drive system of claim 1, wherein the generator is coupled to the electric motor by an insulative shaft. 5. The vehicle system of claim 1, wherein the electric motor interfaces with a crankshaft of the first prime mover, a PTO, or a gear or a flywheel associated with the first prime mover or wherein the electric motor provides power to the crankshaft of the first prime mover through the PTO coupled to a transmission. 6. The vehicle drive system of claim 1, further comprising another electric motor in direct or indirect mechanical communication with the first prime mover; and another high power generator coupled to the another electric motor, wherein the another electric motor is configured to generate energy for storage in the energy 

 source via rotation using power from the first prime mover and drives the another high power generator using energy from the electric energy source. 7. The vehicle drive system of claim 1, further comprising: another electric motor in direct or indirect mechanical communication with the first prime mover, wherein the another electric motor is configured to generate energy for storage in the energy source via rotation using power from the first prime mover and drives other loads using energy from the electric energy source. 8. The vehicle drive system of claim 1, wherein the generator is a 60KW or greater generator. 9. The vehicle drive system of claim 1, further comprising: a power take off configured to be in mechanical communication with the electric motor, and driving the generator using power from the electric motor and the first prime mover to provide surge power. 10. The vehicle drive system of claim 1, wherein driving the generator with the first prime mover when a level of the energy source is below a first level. 11. The vehicle drive system of claim 1, wherein the generator is an embedded transmission integrated generator. 12. A method of operating a vehicle having an at least partially electric vehicle drive system, the vehicle comprising a first prime mover, an electric energy source, and a prime mover driven transmission, the method comprising: operating an electric motor directly or indirectly coupled to the first prime mover via a PTO or an interface in mechanical communication with the first prime mover to drive a high power generator coupled to the electric motor; and uncoupling the electric motor from the first prime mover and operating the electric motor using power from the electric energy source to drive the high power generator. 13. The method of claim 12, further comprising: 

 operating another electric motor directly or indirectly coupled to the first prime mover via another PTO in mechanical communication with the first prime mover to drive another high power generator coupled to the another electric motor; and uncoupling the another electric motor from the first prime mover and operating the another electric motor using power from the electric energy source to drive the high power generator. 14. The method of claim 12, wherein the generator is 60KW generator 15. The method of claim 12, further comprising: operating another electric motor directly or indirectly coupled to the first prime mover via another PTO in mechanical communication with the first prime mover to propel the vehicle without operating the first prime mover. 16. A vehicle drive system for a vehicle comprising a transmission, and a rechargeable electrical energy source, the vehicle drive system comprising: a first electric motor in direct or indirect mechanical communication with the transmission, wherein the first electric motor is configured to provide propel the vehicle using power from the electrical energy source; and a second electric motor in direct or indirect mechanical communication with a high power generator, wherein the second electric motor is configured to rotate the generator using power from the electrical energy source. 17. The vehicle drive system of claim 16, wherein the second electric motor operates at a constant speed. 18. The vehicle drive system of claim 16, further comprising: an engine coupled to the transmission, wherein the first electric motor is rotated using power from the engine to provide power to the electrical energy source. 19. The vehicle system of claim 18, further comprising: an inverter coupled to the electrical energy source, the inverter being lower power than the generator. 20. The vehicle system of claim 16, wherein the second electric motor is coupled to a PTO. 21. A fully electric vehicle, comprising: a traction battery; a DC traction motor for propelling the vehicle using power from the traction battery; and a high power generator coupled to the DC traction motor, wherein the electric motor is configured to generate energy for storage in the traction battery via rotation using power from regenerative braking and drives the generator using energy from the traction battery. 22. The fully electric vehicle of claim 21, further comprising: a secondary battery and a DC to DC converter between the secondary battery and the traction battery. 23. The fully electric vehicle of claim 22, further comprising: a secondary electric motor indirectly or directly coupled to a transmission of the vehicle.
